title: Coherence reframes R1-R5 — triangulation instruments benchmarked on real data
premise: Follow-up to the O1-O5 oscillatory negative. Five reframes that couple the RIGHT signals (internal-vs-stated, ensemble-disagreement, grounding-lock, perturbation-stability, operational-gate) instead of scoring answer-coherence (= confidence). Built + run on real backends, adversarially verified (each verifier hunted for a bug HIDING a positive).

backends.honestyProbe: logistic over Qwen hidden states, DPO honest/deceptive pairs (in-sample AUROC 1.0)

headline: 4 of 5 reframes are honest negatives/weak on real data. ONE — R2, ensemble disagreement as an OOD/abstain signal — is a QUALIFIED POSITIVE: disagreement among domain-specialist probes predicts the ensemble's own errors at AUROC 0.80 (CI excludes chance, 3 seeds), turning the cross-domain-transfer failure into a usable abstention trigger. Caveat: it does not beat the ensemble's confidence margin (0.834). The promising R1 signal at n=160 (+0.07 AUROC) EVAPORATED at n=464 (+0.017, CI includes 0) — a textbook small-sample regression, confirmed by group-aware CV (-0.005). No gate is claimed 'met'; all candidateOnly.

R1_internal_vs_stated.gate: logistic(stated, internal) beats stated-alone at AUROC(correct), paired CI>0, >=2 seeds
R1_internal_vs_stated.adversarial: Sound. -probe reproduces identical delta (LR sign-invariant); group-aware CV -> -0.005 (leakage FAVORED probe); C x class_weight sweep never clears 0. DPO honesty probe carries ~no correctness info on SimpleQA (domain mismatch).
defectsLogged: logprob feature is a dead constant 1.0 (DeepSeek token-confidence saturates) -> stated+logprob delta identically 0
combined-pack dedup was ad-hoc fuzzy (dropped 56 rows, enriched-incorrect); does not affect verdict

R2_ensemble_disagreement_ood.gate: disagreement (std of out-of-domain probes' P) predicts OOD ensemble error, AUROC>0.5 CI excluding chance, >=2 seeds
R2_ensemble_disagreement_ood.adversarial: verdict FLIPPED from the initial 'underpowered': 'correct' label was near-degenerate (3 domains 100% true) so no probes trained; 'accepted' (balanced 27/95) trains 3 probes and the signal clears chance on all seeds. Honest caveat: NOT better than the ensemble margin (paired delta -0.033, CI straddles 0).
R2_ensemble_disagreement_ood.boundary: Usable OOD/abstain signal; requires >=3 both-class domains; on par with (not superior to) the simpler ensemble-margin. Single dataset, n=112.

R3_grounding_lock.gate: marker-free claim<->evidence residual separates supported/unsupported with a real gap
R3_grounding_lock.adversarial: Sound. Marker-stripping complete (22->0); stricter claim-echo removal raises gap to 0.099 not collapse. Real but weak content signal; similarity cannot capture entailment (needs NLI).

R4_perturbation_stability.gate: paraphrase-stability beats temperature self-consistency at AUROC(correct), paired CI>0
R4_perturbation_stability.adversarial: Sound. paraphrase near-zero signal (0.583 correct vs 0.564 wrong) vs self-consistency strong (0.741 vs 0.511). n=56 (paraphrase-gen failed ~half) -> underpowered but point estimate clearly not-positive.

R5_operational_gate.gate: logistic(stated, probe) abstention gate lowers fabrication-AURC vs stated-alone, paired CI>0
R5_operational_gate.adversarial: Sound. Full numeric reproduction; correct AURC/CI polarity. Follows directly from R1's null (probe carries no correctness info).

takeaway: Coupling the right signals mostly did not rescue coherence-as-verification on these datasets — but ONE reframe did: ensemble DISAGREEMENT is an honest OOD/abstain trigger (you never ask a probe to generalize; you ask whether your experts still agree). That aligns with sophia's calibrated-abstention thesis. It is a candidate, not a validated result: it needs >=3 both-class domains, doesn't beat the confidence margin, and is one dataset.
